task_completion.description: The input global countries layer is loaded or selected in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' is visible in the Layers panel or selected as the input in a tool dialog.

task_completion.description: A tool to split the vector layer is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for 'Split Vector Layer' (or an equivalent batch splitting tool) is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The split field is set to 'CONTINENT'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the split tool dialog, the Unique ID field or split field is explicitly set to 'CONTINENT'.

task_completion.description: The output directory is set to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output directory path in the tool dialog is set to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/countries_by_continent/'.

task_completion.description: The resulting GeoJSON files are successfully exported to the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or GIS browser shows multiple GeoJSON files named after continents (e.g., Africa.geojson, Asia.geojson) in the '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/countries_by_continent/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS application's GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIS software dialogs and menus, not terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent used a split tool rather than manually creating files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen configuring and running a split tool, rather than manually selecting features and exporting them one by one.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GIS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a real GIS application (like QGIS) being used, not a text editor or mock interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input dataset contains exactly 8 unique 'CONTINENT' values: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania, Seven seas (open ocean), and South America. The VLM should expect to see exactly 8 GeoJSON files generated in the output directory, one for each of these continents.
